local_names.romanized_local: Cì Guǒ Fān Lì Zhī (Mandarin); Durian Belanda (Malay)

aliases: 红毛榴莲 / 刺果番荔枝 (Cì Guǒ Fān Lì Zhī)

chinese: 红毛榴莲 / 刺果番荔枝 (Cì Guǒ Fān Lì Zhī)

region_of_origin: Native to the Caribbean and Central America; widely cultivated in Southeast Asia (Malaysia, Indonesia, Philippines, Singapore)
food_subgroup: Fresh tropical fruit — dark green spiky skin removed; white fibrous flesh scooped out, seeds discarded

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for soursop (Annona muricata). Closest parent is tropical Annona fruit. No URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Soursop (Annona muricata) — FoodEx2 category: Tropical and subtropical fruits, edible peel removed
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC is the closest defensible parent code for tropical Annona fruits with peel removed. Exact soursop-specific code not confirmed; closest_parent used to avoid fabrication.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No confirmed SNOMED CT code for soursop fruit flesh. Null assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 1 cup (150g) of peeled, seed-free soursop flesh, as commonly consumed fresh or used in juice preparation.

nutrient_source.title: Soursop, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 167762) cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy; FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for soursop raw flesh used as primary nutrient anchor. FAO/INFOODS SEA values cross-referenced for regional alignment. Values are consistent between both sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soursop (Annona muricata) — University of Sydney GI Database context values and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; FAO/INFOODS SEA
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~40 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database context values for Annona species and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference data. No single direct soursop GI trial is published in the University of Sydney database; the value is consistent with the fruit's fibre content (3.3g/100g), moderate sugar profile, and published Annona genus GI context. Confidence 0.9. GL per 150g serving = (40 × 16.8 × 1.5) / 100 = 10.08 ≈ 10.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (14mg/100g), good potassium (278mg/100g) — favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Soursop is suitable for hypertension patients: its low sodium content (14mg/100g) and meaningful potassium (278mg/100g) support a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with blood pressure management guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~40), moderate GL per serving (~10.1), meaningful dietary fibre (3.3g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Soursop has a low GI (~40) and moderate glycaemic load (~10.1 per 150g serving); its fibre content (3.3g/100g) is among the higher values for tropical fruits and provides meaningful glucose moderation, making it a suitable fruit choice for T2DM patients in controlled portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, good potassium and dietary fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Soursop is a heart-friendly fruit: negligible saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, and a good potassium-to-sodium ratio support cardiovascular health; its dietary fibre (3.3g/100g) contributes to LDL cholesterol management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Modest Vitamin C (20.6mg/100g), iron (0.6mg/100g), potassium; no specific RA harm identified
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Soursop flesh presents no specific concern for rheumatoid arthritis patients; its modest Vitamin C and iron content provide general nutritional value, and no RA-aggravating compounds are present in the fruit flesh.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soursop (Annona muricata), known as 刺果番荔枝 or 红毛榴莲 in Chinese, durian belanda or sirsak in Malay/Indonesian, and guanabana in Spanish-speaking regions, is a tropical fruit native to the Caribbean and Central America that has become widely cultivated across Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, Indonesia, the Philippines, and Singapore. The fruit has a distinctive dark green, spiky exterior; the edible portion is the white, fibrous, sweet-sour flesh inside, consumed after removing the skin and discarding the seeds. Its flavour profile is complex — often described as a blend of pineapple, strawberry, and citrus with a creamy undertone. Nutritionally, soursop is a sound tropical fruit choice: 66 kcal per 100g, low GI (~40), moderate glycaemic load (~10.1 per 150g serving), good dietary fibre (3.3g/100g among the higher values for tropical fruits), modest Vitamin C (20.6mg/100g), and meaningful potassium (278mg/100g). Saturated fat is negligible (0.1g/100g) and cholesterol is zero, making it cardiovascular-friendly. A critical clinical distinction must be made: the FRUIT FLESH is safe and nutritious, but SOURSOP LEAVES are widely marketed — particularly in Singapore Chinese medical halls and online — as a cancer cure. These claims have no rigorous clinical evidence. Furthermore, soursop leaves contain annonacin, a mitochondrial complex I inhibitor associated with atypical Parkinsonism (resembling Progressive Supranuclear Palsy) with chronic consumption. Patients and consumers should be clearly advised that safe fruit consumption and potentially harmful soursop leaf tea use are entirely different matters. In Singapore, soursop is sold at wet markets and supermarkets and is commonly consumed fresh, as juice, or in desserts.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (exact match for soursop raw flesh) cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database context values for Annona species, with confidence 0.9. All mandatory multilingual names are present.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for soursop not reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 167762) or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source used for this record. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — soursop is a raw fruit with no industrial trans fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — soursop is a plant-based fruit). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based food contains no B12). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — soursop is not a significant dietary source of vitamin D, confirmed per USDA SR Legacy). GI value of ~40 is an analogous estimate; no single direct soursop GI clinical trial is available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible parent for tropical Annona fruits; exact soursop-specific FoodEx2 code not confirmed. IMPORTANT CLINICAL NOTE: This record covers soursop FRUIT FLESH only. Soursop leaf products (teas, extracts) are outside the scope of this food record and carry separate safety concerns including annonacin-associated neurotoxicity risk.
